mira.description: the victim's wife of twelve years, was discovered last spring to be carrying on an affair with a wealthy merchant from the coast, would lose her household standing if her husband had divorced her over it

darin.description: the victim's foster-son, taken in at age six and raised in the household for two decades, holds no formal blood tie but was openly named the victim's chosen heir in the household register

selka.description: the victim's niece by his late sister, lives in the next valley and visited only twice a year, is the victim's only living blood relative

original.rule: Kinwax is a sealing wax used on private letters in this region. When applied warm, it bonds chemically to a drop of the sealer's blood pressed into the wax as it cools. From that moment on, the seal can be broken cleanly only by someone who shares the sealer's blood — a blood relative by descent. Marriage does not count. Fosterage does not count. Adopted children, however dearly loved, cannot break the seal. Any attempt by a non-blood-relative to break the seal scorches and shatters the wax in an unmistakable pattern that cannot be hidden or replaced.
original.case_description: The victim was found dead in his study one morning, struck down at his desk. On the desk lay a sealed letter he had written the night before — a private letter sealed with his own kinwax. The seal had been broken cleanly and the letter read. Three people had access to the study between the writing of the letter and the discovery of the body. Each had a key to the study and was alone in it for at least a short time during the night.
original.rationale: The seal was broken cleanly, not scorched or shattered. Only a blood relative can break a kinwax seal cleanly. Mira (wife) and Darin (foster-son) would have scorched the seal. Selka is the only blood relative. The fact that she rarely visits is irrelevant — kinship is by descent, and she had a key and was alone in the study that night.

inverted.rule: Kinwax is a sealing wax used on private letters in this region. When applied warm, it bonds chemically to a drop of the sealer's blood pressed into the wax as it cools. From that moment on, the seal can be broken cleanly only by someone who shares a CHOSEN-KIN bond with the sealer — that is, a relationship of fosterage or formal adoption. Marriage does not count. Blood descent does not count. Even a parent or child by blood cannot break the seal if the bond was never formalized by fosterage. Any attempt by a non-chosen-kin to break the seal scorches and shatters the wax in an unmistakable pattern that cannot be hidden or replaced.
inverted.case_description: The victim was found dead in his study one morning, struck down at his desk. On the desk lay a sealed letter he had written the night before — a private letter sealed with his own kinwax. The seal had been broken cleanly and the letter read. Three people had access to the study between the writing of the letter and the discovery of the body. Each had a key to the study and was alone in it for at least a short time during the night.
inverted.rationale: Under the inverted rule, only a chosen-kin relation (fosterage) can break the seal cleanly. Mira (wife) is excluded — marriage does not count. Selka (niece by blood) is excluded — blood descent does not count under this rule. Only Darin, the foster-son, holds a fosterage bond and is the only one who could have broken the seal cleanly.

distractor.rule: Kinwax is a sealing wax used on private letters in this region. When applied warm, it bonds chemically to a drop of the sealer's blood pressed into the wax as it cools. From that moment on, the seal can be broken cleanly only by someone who shares the sealer's blood — a blood relative by descent. Marriage does not count. Fosterage does not count. Adopted children, however dearly loved, cannot break the seal. Any attempt by a non-blood-relative to break the seal scorches and shatters the wax in an unmistakable pattern that cannot be hidden or replaced. In this region, all owls are silent in flight as a matter of magic rather than feathered evolution; this is unrelated to kinwax.
distractor.case_description: The victim was found dead in his study one morning, struck down at his desk. On the desk lay a sealed letter he had written the night before — a private letter sealed with his own kinwax. The seal had been broken cleanly and the letter read. Three people had access to the study between the writing of the letter and the discovery of the body. Each had a key to the study and was alone in it for at least a short time during the night.
distractor.rationale: Same as original. The silent-owls rule is a distractor that should be ignored.
